evidence.excerpt: Document Dow Release 1 59 214434 Sp 16 7 18 1963 OCEICIAI a OA) <sttTivinc JL onLy- AFUE eR EXECUTIVE ie OF THE PRESIDENT NATIONAL AERONAUTICS and SPACE COUNCIL WASHINGTON July 18, 1963 pe, aR af } By - a 2 MEMORANDUM FOR \ Mr. Robert F, Packard aw Office of International Scientific Affairs Department of State Washington 25, D.C, SUBJECT: Thoughts on the Space Alien Race Question - Oe During recent discussions the question has occasionally, though 4. rarely, arisen that perhaps we should consider the policy question of what to do if an alien intelligence is discovered in space. Some discussion of this occurred, as you will recall, during deliberations on BNSP TaskI, This memo contains some miscellaneous thoughts on the question, The consensus of scientific view says, with quite good reasons, that the possibility of running across an alien intelligent race in our solar system 78 negligibl

evidence.transcript: Document Dow Release 1 255 413270 Ufos And Defense What Should We Prepare For
evidence.excerpt: Chapter 3, I - intimidation maneuvers against aircraft as in Luxeuil and Tehran (Chapters 1.1 and 2.3), - witnesses paralyzed, engines shutting off, lights going out (San Carlos de Bariloche, I Chapter 2.5). The advances that have been made in the conquest ofspace and in the development of nuclear technology could be troubling them. Wouldn't it be logical to think that these I extraterrestrial civilizations have established stations, even colonies, in the asteroid belt and why not relay stations on the moon? Our forays and projects studied in the United difying the orbits ofasteroids using H bombs in order to bring them closer to States for mo I the earth's orbit for mining purposes could be disturbing them. For the moment, they do not appear to be meddling in our affairs, but it is advisable to ask ourselves what they are actually seeking. Do they want to invade earth? To preserve it fr

Thus immediately following what would later become the Roswell affair, General Twining was tasked with preparing a secret report on "flying disks, -the existence ofwhich was not revealed until 22 years later in the Condon report. I It emerges from this that these objects truly do exist. But since then the United States has followed a policy ofincreasing secrecy ( classification above "top secret " ofcertain UFO I files, according to General Bany Goldwater) and constant disinformation.
